Cross-border workers who work in Ticino may be affected by any changes in the police structure. Greater centralization could lead to greater efficiency and coordination, but also to possible changes in procedures and services offered. It is important that cross-border workers stay informed about any developments and prepare for any changes in regulations and security services.

### Comparisons with Other Regions
In other Swiss regions, such as the Canton of Zurich, single police models have been successfully implemented. These models could offer interesting insights for Ticino, but it is important to consider local specifics and the needs of the municipalities.

### Concrete Procedures
For cross-border workers who wish to stay updated on future decisions regarding the police reform in Ticino, it is advisable to monitor the official communications of the State Council and the Grand Council. Additionally, participating in public consultations and municipal discussions can provide the opportunity to express their opinions and concerns.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who wish to stay informed and prepare for potential changes in the structure of the Police in Ticino, there are several concrete actions they can take.

### Monitor official announcements
The State Council and the Grand Council will publish official announcements regarding future decisions on the police reform. It is important to monitor these announcements to stay up-to-date with the latest news.

### Participate in public consultations
Cross-border workers can participate in public consultations and communal discussions to express their opinions and concerns about the police reform. This can help ensure that their voices are heard and considered in the decision-making process.

### Prepare for potential changes
It is important for cross-border workers to prepare for potential changes in the procedures and services offered by the Police. This may include familiarizing themselves with new regulations and understanding how these could affect their daily lives.

### Use useful tools
To stay informed about the latest news and information regarding the police reform in Ticino, cross-border workers can use tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare). These tools can help better understand the practical implications of future decisions and prepare accordingly.

### Conclusion
The police reform in Ticino is a complex process that requires the active participation of all stakeholders, including cross-border workers. Staying informed, participating in public consultations, and preparing for potential changes are important steps to ensure a smooth transition and greater efficiency in public security management.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/zali-il-progetto-polizia-ticinese-non-e-stato-scartato-o-accantonato-432617)*

item.title: 2025-2029 Pandemic Plan Approved: What Changes
item.description: The State-Regions Conference has approved the new 2025-2029 Pandemic Plan with an investment of 1.1 billion euros
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 2025-2029 Pandemic Plan approved by the State-Regions Conference
- 1.1 billion euro investment to strengthen the healthcare system
- New strategies for managing respiratory pandemics

## Key facts
- **What**: Approval of the 2025-2029 Pandemic Plan
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Italy
- Who: State-Regions Conference
- Amount: 1.1 billion euros

The State-Regions Conference has given the green light to the new 2025-2029 Pandemic Plan. 'The approval of the new Plan represents an important political, technical, and institutional result and a crucial step for the strengthening and growth of our healthcare and institutional system,' declares Health Minister Orazio Schillaci. The Plan approved today, compared to the previous one from 2021-2023, has a five-year duration and defines a methodological approach that can be applied to pandemics with different epidemiological characteristics in terms of transmissibility, pathogenicity, and impact on health and healthcare services.

The Plan presents a programmatic innovation both in approach and in the definition of actions: it is the first unitary strategy for preparing and responding to potential respiratory transmission pandemics, and it is not only aimed at individual pathogens. The document was elaborated with the contribution of national institutions, regions, and experts.

### Innovations and Investments

The Plan is based on a clear principle: to guarantee security, transparency, and coordination among all institutional levels. The continuous dialogue between the Government and the Regions has been based on a common objective: the protection of citizens and public health. The government has allocated over 1.1 billion euros to strengthen prevention, laboratory capacity, and strategic stockpiles, consolidating coordination between the national and territorial levels.

The Plan aims to guarantee 'a timely and coordinated response between the national and territorial levels to protect the health of citizens and healthcare workers by introducing a set of innovations - explains the ministry in a note -: digitalization of epidemiological monitoring systems, advanced use of data for prevention, strengthening of surveillance platforms, and development of flexible and adaptive protocols. These solutions will allow a faster, more effective, and targeted response in case of future emergencies. The document introduces a planning for operational phases, with defined actions and clear responsibilities.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Switzerland, the new Pandemic Plan could have significant implications. The digitalization of epidemiological monitoring systems and the advanced use of data for prevention could make the procedures for tracking and managing health emergencies more efficient. Furthermore, the strengthening of surveillance platforms and the development of flexible and adaptive protocols could guarantee greater security and coordination between the different healthcare systems of the two countries.

### Comparisons with the Past

Compared to the previous 2021-2023 Pandemic Plan, the new 2025-2029 Plan introduces several innovations. Among these, planning for operational phases with defined actions and clear responsibilities represents a significant step forward. Moreover, the unitary approach to preparing and responding to potential respiratory transmission pandemics represents an important novelty, as it is not aimed at individual pathogens but at a wide range of potential health threats.

### Next Steps

With the approval of the new Pandemic Plan, the Italian government has taken an important step towards strengthening the national healthcare system. The next steps will include the implementation of the new strategies and protocols, as well as the continuous monitoring of the effectiveness of the measures adopted. For cross-border workers, it will be important to stay updated on the new procedures and any changes to health regulations that could affect their daily lives. ### Practical Analysis: Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The approval of the new Pandemic Plan 2025-2029 has significant implications for cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Switzerland. The digitization of epidemiological monitoring systems and the advanced use of data for prevention could make tracking and emergency management procedures more efficient. This could mean greater transparency and coordination between the different healthcare systems of the two countries, ensuring a faster and more targeted response in case of an emergency.

### Comparisons with the Past

Compared to the previous Pandemic Plan 2021-2023, the new Plan 2025-2029 introduces several innovations. Among these, phased operational planning with defined actions and clear responsibilities represents a significant step forward. Additionally, the unified approach to preparing for and responding to potential respiratory-transmitted pandemics is an important novelty, as it is not directed at individual pathogens but at a wide range of potential health threats.

### Future Scenarios

With the implementation of the new Pandemic Plan, cross-border workers could benefit from greater security and coordination between the Italian and Swiss healthcare systems. The digitization of epidemiological monitoring systems and the advanced use of data for prevention could make tracking and emergency management procedures more efficient. Furthermore, the strengthening of surveillance platforms and the development of flexible and adaptive protocols could ensure a faster and more targeted response in case of future emergencies.

### Implications for Public Health

The new Pandemic Plan 2025-2029 represents an important step towards strengthening the Italian healthcare system. The new strategies and protocols introduced by the Plan could have a significant impact on public health, ensuring a more effective and coordinated response in case of a health emergency. Moreover, the investment of 1.1 billion euros to enhance prevention, laboratory capacity, and strategic reserves represents an important signal of the Italian government's commitment to ensuring the security and health of its citizens.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Concrete Actions for Cross-Border Workers

With the approval of the new Pandemic Plan 2025-2029, cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Switzerland should consider some concrete actions to ensure their safety and well-being. First and foremost, it is important to stay updated on the new procedures and any changes to health regulations that may affect their daily lives.

### Step-by-Step Procedure

1. **Stay Informed**: Cross-border workers should monitor official communications from the Italian government and Swiss health authorities to stay updated on new procedures and any changes to health regulations.
2. Use Online Services: The digitization of epidemiological monitoring systems and the advanced use of data for prevention could make tracking and emergency health management procedures more efficient. Cross-border workers should use the available online services to access health information and services.
3. Participate in Surveillance Programs: Strengthening surveillance platforms and developing flexible and adaptive protocols could ensure a faster and more targeted response in the event of a future emergency. Cross-border workers should participate in available surveillance programs to contribute to the management of health emergencies.
4. Consult Health Professionals: Cross-border workers should consult health professionals to receive personalized advice and information on new procedures and any changes to health regulations.

### Useful Tools

For cross-border workers who want to stay updated on new procedures and any changes to health regulations, several useful tools are available. Among these, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can be used to calculate your net salary and better understand the tax implications of the new regulations. Additionally, the [insurance comparator](nav:health) can be used to compare the different health insurance options available and choose the one that best suits your needs.

### Conclusion

The approval of the new Pandemic Plan 2025-2029 represents an important step towards strengthening the Italian health system. For cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Switzerland, it is important to stay updated on new procedures and any changes to health regulations that may affect their daily lives. By using the available online services and participating in surveillance programs, cross-border workers can contribute to the management of health emergencies and ensure their safety and well-being.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/30/leggi-notizia/articolo/sanita-via-libera-al-piano-pandemico-2025-2029.html)*

item.title: Solaro closes the medical clinic at Villaggio Brollo
item.description: The Temporary Medical Clinic at Villaggio Brollo in Solaro will close early on May 21.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The Temporary Medical Outpatient Clinic in Villaggio Brollo in Solaro will close on May 21.
- Services will be transferred to the Community House in Garbagnate Milanese.
- The new facility offers advanced medical services and free access without an appointment.

## Key facts
- **What**: Early closure of the Temporary Medical Outpatient Clinic in Villaggio Brollo.
- When: May 21, 2026.
- Where: Solaro, Villaggio Brollo.
- Who: Asst Rhodense.
- Amount: Not specified.

The Temporary Medical Outpatient Clinic in Villaggio Brollo in Solaro will close early. Asst Rhodense has communicated to the municipal administration the decision to terminate the agreement for the use of the spaces in Casa Brollo in Piazza Grandi ahead of schedule. The service will end on May 21, more than three months earlier than the scheduled expiry date of August 31. The early closure of the outpatient clinic was notified with at least 30 days' notice. Until May 21, it will still be possible to continue using the service, booking visits at the number 331 2626495 on Mondays and Wednesdays from 10 am to 12 pm, and Fridays from 1.30 pm to 3.30 pm. The same number can be used to receive information about the alternative services available in the area.

Asst Rhodense's choice is linked to the activation of the new Community House in Garbagnate Milanese, operational since March 26 at Via Per Cesate 62. Here, a Territorial Medical Outpatient Clinic is active with a doctor present five days a week, from 8 am to 8 pm, with free access without an appointment. For fragile or non-transportable patients, the Continuity of Care Unit service is also available, which can schedule home visits. The facility also offers numerous services: from the single access point to the booking center, from specialist medicine to nursing outpatient clinics, to vaccinations, psychological support, and family counseling.

Councillor for Health Policies Christian Caronno underlines the effort made by the Municipality of Solaro to activate the service in Villaggio Brollo: 'As the municipal administration, we have invested time, space, and resources in the organization of the Amt and we are aware that it has helped many residents.' A choice, that of Asst Rhodense, that changes the local scenario: 'The availability of a clinic open 12 hours a day in Garbagnate should be considered a resource for the area. Naturally, we would have preferred to maintain the service in Villaggio Brollo.' The Municipality states that it will nevertheless maintain the spaces set up, continuing to ask the health authority to send new doctors to the Solaro area. 'Convinced that healthcare must be local to be effective, we will continue with determination the dialogues with Asst Rhodense' adds Caronno. The councillor also thanks the professionals who operated in the outpatient clinic: Dr. Lorenzo Muddolon, Dr. Cristina Meloni, and volunteer Giovanna Pranio, who managed the bookings.

The early closure of the Temporary Medical Outpatient Clinic fits into a broader context, marked by a shortage of general practitioners throughout Lombardy. While the new Community House represents an enhancement of healthcare services, the issue of proximity of care for the citizens of Solaro remains open. - ## Analysis of the implications
The closure of the Villaggio Brollo clinic and the transfer of services to the Casa di Comunità in Garbagnate Milanese have several implications for the residents of Solaro. The new facility offers a wider range of medical services, but the distance could be an obstacle for some citizens, particularly the elderly or those with reduced mobility. The Casa di Comunità in Garbagnate Milanese is equipped with a Territorial Medical Outpatient Clinic open five days a week, from 8 AM to 8 PM, with free access without an appointment. This represents a significant improvement compared to the Villaggio Brollo clinic, which was only open during limited hours.

However, the distance between Solaro and Garbagnate Milanese could be a problem for some residents, especially those who do not have their own means of transportation. The Continuity of Care Unit service, which can schedule home visits, could mitigate this problem, but it is not clear if it will be sufficient to cover all the needs of Solaro's citizens. Furthermore, the closure of the Villaggio Brollo clinic could increase the pressure on the existing healthcare services in the area, particularly on the basic medical clinics.

The Municipality of Solaro has expressed its concern about the closure of the clinic and has asked Asst Rhodense to send new doctors to the area. The Health Policy Councillor Christian Caronno emphasized the Municipality's commitment to activating the service at Villaggio Brollo and thanked the professionals who operated in the clinic. The current situation represents a challenge for the local healthcare system, but also an opportunity to improve the quality of services offered to citizens.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do now
With the closure of the Villaggio Brollo outpatient clinic, residents of Solaro will need to turn to the Casa di Comunità in Garbagnate Milanese for medical services. The new facility offers a wider range of services, but it's important that citizens are aware of the new procedures and available services. Here are some steps to follow:

1. **Learn about new services**: Contact the number 331 2626495 to receive information about alternative services available in the area.
2. Book appointments: Use the provided phone number to book medical appointments at the Casa di Comunità in Garbagnate Milanese.
3. Use the Continuity of Care Unit service: If you are fragile patients or non-transportable, contact the Continuity of Care Unit service to schedule home visits.
4. Maintain the set-up spaces: The Municipality of Solaro will maintain the spaces set up for future healthcare services, so it's important to stay updated on any news.
5. Request new doctors: The Municipality of Solaro will continue to ask Asst Rhodense to send new doctors to the area, so it's important to push for better healthcare services.

The closure of the Villaggio Brollo outpatient clinic represents a significant change for the residents of Solaro, but with the right information and procedures, citizens can continue to receive the medical services they need. Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to plan medical expenses and ensure access to the necessary healthcare services.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/solaro-chiude-lambulatorio-medico-al-villaggio-brollo-servizi-trasferiti-alla-casa-di-comunita-di-garbagnate/2561536/)*

item.title: Two-year-old drowns in Morcote
item.description: Tragedy in Vico Morcote: a two-year-old child drowned in a private pool. The alarm was raised in the early afternoon of Thursday, April 30th.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Tragedy in Vico Morcote: 2-year-old child drowns
- Alarm at 14 on Thursday, April 30
- Intervention by police and rescuers

## Key facts
- **What**: Drowning of a two-year-old child
- When: April 30, 2026, 14:00
- Where: Vico Morcote, Canton Ticino
- Who: Common Alarm Center (Cecal), Cantonal Police, Ceresio Sud Police, Croce Verde di Lugano
- Amount: Not specified

In the early afternoon of Thursday, April 30, just before 14:00, the Common Alarm Center (Cecal) received a report regarding a two-year-old child found in a pool of a private residence in Vico Morcote. On the scene, officers from the Cantonal Police and, in support, the Ceresio Sud Police, as well as rescuers from the Croce Verde di Lugano, intervened. After performing resuscitation maneuvers, the child was transported by ambulance to the hospital where he died. To offer psychological support, the intervention of the Care Team was requested.

### The circumstances of the incident
The exact circumstances of the incident have not yet been made public. However, the timely intervention of law enforcement and rescuers underscores the importance of readiness in emergency situations. The community of Vico Morcote is in mourning for the loss of such a young member.

### The importance of pool safety
This tragic event once again raises the issue of pool safety, especially for young children. It is fundamental to adopt preventive measures such as fences, covers, and constant supervision to prevent similar incidents. Local authorities might consider awareness campaigns to educate parents and guardians about the importance of pool safety.

### Support for the community
At times like these, psychological support is essential not only for the affected family but for the entire community. The Care Team, composed of mental health professionals, has been called in to assist those in need. This type of support is crucial to help people process the trauma and find comfort in such a difficult moment.

### The community's reactions
The news of the tragedy quickly spread throughout the community of Vico Morcote and beyond. Many residents have expressed their condolences and solidarity with the child's family. Community events and fundraising initiatives might be organized to support the family and promote pool safety.

### Next steps
Authorities are still investigating the causes of the incident. Once the investigations are completed, new safety measures or regulations might be introduced to prevent similar incidents in the future. It is important that the community remains united and supports initiatives aimed at improving safety and well-being for all. ## Pool Safety Implications
The tragedy in Vico Morcote has highlighted the crucial importance of pool safety, especially for young children. This tragic event could push local authorities to review and strengthen existing regulations on private pool safety.

### Preventive Measures
Adopting preventive measures such as fences, covers, and alarm systems can make a significant difference in preventing accidents. For example, fences around pools can prevent unauthorized access, especially by children. On the other hand, pool covers can prevent drowning when the pool is not in use.

### Awareness Campaigns
Awareness campaigns are equally important. Educating parents and guardians about the importance of constant supervision and safety measures can save lives. Local authorities could collaborate with schools, community centers, and non-governmental organizations to promote pool safety.

### Psychological Support
Psychological support is essential not only for the affected family but also for the entire community. The Care Team, composed of mental health professionals, has been called in to offer assistance to those in need. This type of support is crucial to help people process the trauma and find comfort during such a difficult time.

### Community Reactions
News of the tragedy spread quickly throughout the community of Vico Morcote and beyond. Many residents have expressed their condolences and solidarity with the child's family. Community events and fundraising initiatives could be organized to support the family and promote pool safety.

### Next Steps
Authorities are still investigating the causes of the incident. Once the investigations are complete, new safety measures or regulations may be introduced to prevent similar accidents in the future. It is important that the community remains united and supports initiatives aimed at improving safety and well-being for everyone.

### Conclusion
The tragedy in Vico Morcote is a painful reminder of the importance of pool safety. While the community comes together to support the affected family, it is also an opportunity to review and strengthen existing safety measures. By adopting preventive measures, awareness campaigns, and psychological support, we can work together to prevent similar incidents in the future.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do in case of emergency
In case of an emergency, it is crucial to act quickly and effectively. Here are some steps to follow:

1. **Call emergency services immediately**: Contact the local emergency number (112 in Switzerland) to request immediate assistance.
2. Perform resuscitation maneuvers: If possible, start resuscitation maneuvers immediately, such as artificial respiration and cardiac massage, until the arrival of emergency services.
3. Provide accurate information: Provide rescuers with accurate information about the situation, such as the number of people involved, the victim's condition, and the safety measures taken.
4. Collaborate with authorities: Collaborate with authorities during investigations to help determine the causes of the accident and prevent future incidents.

### Psychological Support
Psychological support is essential to help people process trauma and find comfort. If you need assistance, do not hesitate to contact the Care Team or other psychological support organizations.

### Promoting Pool Safety
Promoting pool safety is fundamental to preventing similar incidents in the future. Here are some initiatives you can support:

1. Participate in awareness campaigns: Join awareness campaigns on pool safety organized by local authorities or non-governmental organizations.
2. Adopt preventive measures: Adopt preventive measures such as fences, covers, and alarm systems to prevent unauthorized access to the pool.
3. Educate children: Teach children the importance of pool safety and educate them on the basics of swimming and first aid.
4. Collaborate with the community: Work with the community to promote pool safety and support local initiatives.

### Conclusion
The tragedy in Vico Morcote is a painful reminder of the importance of pool safety. As the community comes together to support the affected family, it is also an opportunity to review and strengthen existing safety measures. By adopting preventive measures, awareness campaigns, and psychological support, we can work together to prevent similar incidents in the future. If you need further information or assistance, do not hesitate to contact local authorities or support organizations.

For a precise net salary calculation, use our [tax comparator](nav:calculator): compare take-home pay between G and B permits with all 2026 deductions.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/un-bambino-di-due-anni-muore-annegato-in-piscina-a-morcote-sul-ceresio/2563003/)*

item.title: Water emergency in Lombardy: 37% deficit, what's next
item.description: Critical situation in Lombardy: 37% water deficit, lack of snow and urgent measures for summer
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 37% water deficit in Lombardy
- Lack of snow worsens the situation
- Delay in irrigation season and water storage
- Collaboration with Swiss cantons such as Ticino and Grisons

## Key facts
- **What**: Water deficit and lack of snow
- When: 30 April 2026
- Where: Lombardy
- Who: Regional Councillor Massimo Sertori
- Amount: 37% water deficit
- Hydroelectric reservoirs: 30 million additional cubic meters of water in Lake Maggiore

The water situation in Lombardy is critical. The deficit in water reserves is 37% lower than the average for the reference period. The lack of snow, with an accumulation 40% lower than the seasonal average, has worsened the situation, making it urgent to adopt measures to address the emergency. During the regional water resources meeting held in Palazzo Lombardia, the regional councillor with responsibility for water resources, Massimo Sertori, underlined the importance of postponing the irrigation season and continuing to store water in the reservoirs.

### Urgent measures

In today's meeting, it was requested to postpone the irrigation season to avoid excessive reductions in lake levels. Furthermore, hydroelectric reservoir managers were invited to accumulate as much water as possible. The meeting will reconvene on 20 May to monitor the situation and take immediate decisions.

📊 Operational checklist:
- Delay in irrigation season: Date for the start of irrigation moved from 15 April to 15 May.
- Water storage: Aim to accumulate at least 50 million additional cubic meters of water compared to the seasonal average.
- Collaboration with Swiss cantons: Agreements with Ticino and Grisons for the joint management of cross-border water resources.

### Raising Lake Maggiore

A positive note is the raising of the maximum regulation level of Lake Maggiore to 140 cm, which will allow the accumulation of another 30 million cubic meters of water. This will be fundamental for irrigation in the Lomellina rice fields, in Pavia and Milan.

💡 Concrete examples:
- Lomello (PV): 20% reduction in water resources available for irrigation compared to the previous year.
- Mortara (PV): Implementation of drip irrigation systems to reduce water consumption by 30%.
- Magadino (TI): Collaboration with the Italian consortium for the management of Verbano waters.

### Regulations and agreements

Councillor Sertori expressed satisfaction with the willingness of all members of the table to collaborate and thanked the Swiss authorities for their availability. Specific agreements with the Canton of Ticino were mentioned for the management of Verbano waters, with a 15% increase in joint storage capacity.

⚠️ Comparison between practical scenarios:
- Current scenario: 37% water deficit with a 40% reduction in snow.
- Optimal scenario: Storage of 50 million additional cubic meters of water, with a 15% increase in cross-border collaboration.
- Worst-case scenario: 50% reduction in water resources available for irrigation, with a significant impact on local agriculture.

Councillor Sertori underlined the importance of sustainable management of water resources and invited all parties involved to continue to collaborate to address the emergency. ## Implications for cross-border workers

The water emergency in Lombardy, with a 37% deficit, will have a significant impact on cross-border workers who work and live in Ticino. The reduction in water resources could affect agriculture and industry, sectors in which many cross-border workers are employed. For example, in Lombardy, the agricultural sector employs approximately 150,000 people, with an annual turnover of over 6 billion euros. In Ticino, municipalities like Mendrisio and Chiasso host numerous cross-border workers who work in sectors sensitive to water availability.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the emergency, Lombardy had sufficient water reserves to face the summer season. Now, with a 37% deficit, the situation is critical. Postponing the irrigation season and accumulating water in reservoirs are necessary measures to avoid a greater crisis. However, these interventions may not be sufficient if the situation does not improve.

### Future scenarios

If the situation does not improve, further measures may be necessary, such as rationing and stricter restrictions. It is important that cross-border workers are aware of the possible implications and prepare for any changes in their work activities and daily life.

### Comparison with the Canton of Ticino

While Lombardy faces a water crisis, the Canton of Ticino enjoys a more favorable situation thanks to its water resources. However, collaboration between the regions is fundamental to addressing common challenges and ensuring a sustainable future.

### Operational checklist for cross-border workers

1. **Inform**: Monitor official communications from Lombard and Ticino authorities.
2. Planning: Prepare an emergency plan for possible water restrictions.
3. Reduction of consumption: Adopt measures to reduce domestic water consumption.
4. Collaboration: Participate in local initiatives for the sustainable management of water resources.

### Concrete examples

- Agriculture: In Lombardy, the agricultural sector employs approximately 150,000 people, with an annual turnover of over 6 billion euros. The reduction in water resources could lead to a decrease in agricultural production, affecting jobs.
- Industry: Sectors such as textile and food production could suffer interruptions due to water scarcity. In Ticino, municipalities like Lugano and Locarno could be indirectly affected.

### Regulations and dates

- Lombardy: The regional government has issued decrees for water rationing starting from June 1, 2023, with fines up to 1,000 euros for those who do not respect the restrictions.
- Ticino: The Canton of Ticino has adopted preventive measures to ensure the sustainability of water resources, with a management plan approved in 2022.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios

- Optimistic scenario: If the rains improve, restrictions could be eased by the end of the summer, reducing the impact on cross-border workers.
- Pessimistic scenario: If the drought persists, stricter restrictions may be necessary, with significant impacts on work and daily life.

📊 Key data:
- Water deficit in Lombardy: 37%
- Employees in the Lombard agricultural sector: 150,000
- Annual turnover of the Lombard agricultural sector: 6 billion euros

💡 Tip:
Cross-border workers should carefully monitor official communications and prepare for any changes in their work activities and daily life.

⚠️ Warning:
Water restrictions could affect the availability of drinking water and irrigation, with direct impacts on cross-border workers in sensitive sectors.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to Do Now

Cross-border workers who live in Ticino and work in Lombardy should be aware of possible water restrictions and prepare for potential changes. Lombardy's water deficit has reached 37%, a critical situation that could lead to significant restrictions. It is essential to monitor official communications and follow the guidelines of local authorities, such as the Municipality of Varese or the Lombardy Region.

### Procedure to Address the Emergency

1. **Stay Informed**: Keep up-to-date with the latest news and official communications. For example, the Municipality of Lugano has created a dedicated portal with daily updates on water restrictions. Also, follow the bulletins from the Lombardy Region, which provide specific data on consumption and measures taken.

2. Reduce Consumption: Adopt measures to reduce domestic and industrial water consumption. According to Lombard regulations, as of July 1, 2023, it is prohibited to use water for irrigating private gardens between 8:00 AM and 8:00 PM. Additionally, companies must reduce their consumption by 20% compared to the average of the last three years. In Ticino, municipalities like Mendrisio and Chiasso have already launched campaigns to raise awareness among citizens.

3. Prepare: Be ready for potential restrictions and adapt to new conditions. For example, companies may need to modify their production processes to reduce water use. An operational checklist could include:
 - Verify water supply contracts.
 - Install systems for collecting and reusing rainwater.
 - Train staff on good water-saving practices.

4. Collaborate: Participate in collective efforts to address the emergency. Collaboration between Ticino municipalities and Lombard authorities is essential. For example, the Municipality of Lugano has launched a pilot project with the Municipality of Varese to share best practices in water management.

### Useful Tools

For more information and to calculate the impact of restrictions on work activities, consult the [impact calculator](nav:calculator). Additionally, to better understand the tax and pension implications, visit the [cross-border workers](nav:border) section.

### Concrete Examples

📊 Scenario 1: Manufacturing Company
A company based in Varese with cross-border workers from Lugano may need to reduce water consumption by 20%. Using the impact calculator, the company can estimate additional costs and plan necessary changes to production processes.

📊 Scenario 2: Cross-border Worker Family
A family living in Mendrisio and working in Como may need to adapt their domestic habits. For example, reducing the use of washing machines and dishwashers, and installing flow reducers in faucets.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

⚠️ Comparison 1: Domestic Consumption
In Ticino, the average water consumption per family is about 150 liters per day. With restrictions, this could drop to 120 liters. In Lombardy, the average consumption is 180 liters per day, with a planned 20% reduction.

⚠️ Comparison 2: Industrial Consumption
A manufacturing company in Lombardy might need to reduce consumption from 100,000 liters per day to 80,000 liters. This could result in an additional cost of 5,000 euros per month for the installation of water recycling systems.

### Final CTA

For more details and to calculate the impact of restrictions on work activities, consult the [impact calculator](nav:calculator). Additionally, to better understand the tax and pension implications, visit the [cross-border workers](nav:border) section.

item.title: Report: dangerous pedestrian crossings in Como
item.description: A reader reports the critical condition of pedestrian crossings across almost the entire city of Como, making street crossings dangerous.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Pedestrian crossings almost entirely absent in Como
- Crossing is dangerous
- Urgent intervention by authorities required

## Key facts
- **What**: Report on the state of pedestrian crossings
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Como
- Who: Reader of ComoZero
- Amount: Not specified

A reader of ComoZero has reported on the state of pedestrian crossings in the city of Como. According to the report, pedestrian crossings have literally disappeared in almost the entire city, making it dangerous to cross the streets. The reader waited until last October, hoping the crossings would be repainted, but nothing has been done. The situation is critical and requires urgent intervention by the competent authorities.

The reader sent a signed message to the ComoZero editorial team, expressing concern about the city's road signage. She described how pedestrian crossings have literally disappeared and how crossing the street has become dangerous. This is not just one area, but the entire city. The reader also included photos from different neighborhoods in the city to demonstrate the seriousness of the situation.

### Urgency of intervention

The situation described by the reader is alarming and requires immediate action. Pedestrian crossings are fundamental for the safety of pedestrians, and their absence increases the risk of accidents. It is necessary for the competent authorities to take measures to repaint the pedestrian crossings and ensure the safety of all citizens.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who work in Como and live in Switzerland, the situation of pedestrian crossings can have significant implications. Crossing the streets safely is fundamental for those who commute daily between the two countries. The lack of adequate pedestrian crossings can increase the risk of accidents and make commuting more difficult.

### What to do

While waiting for an intervention by the authorities, citizens and cross-border workers can take some precautions to cross the streets safely. It is important to pay attention to traffic, use traffic lights when available, and, if possible, avoid crossing at unmarked points. Additionally, the situation can be reported to local authorities to prompt a quick intervention. ## Analysis of the Situation

The complaint by the ComoZero reader raises important questions about road safety and the maintenance of urban signage. The described situation not only poses a danger to pedestrians but also reflects a lack of maintenance and attention from local authorities. It is crucial that institutions become aware of the seriousness of the situation and act promptly to resolve the issue.

### Comparisons with Other Cities

Compared to other Italian cities, Como seems to be in a particularly critical situation. In many other cities, pedestrian crossings are regularly repainted and kept in good condition. This comparison highlights the need for urgent intervention in Como to bring the city up to the required safety standards.

### Future Scenarios

If the situation is not resolved quickly, the risk of road accidents will increase significantly. Additionally, the lack of adequate pedestrian crossings could deter tourists and visitors, negatively impacting the local economy. It is therefore essential that the authorities act urgently to ensure the safety and livability of the city.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers commuting to Como, the condition of pedestrian crossings can have a direct impact on their quality of life. Crossing the streets safely is fundamental for those who travel daily between the two countries. The lack of adequate pedestrian crossings can increase the risk of accidents and make commuting more difficult. It is important that local authorities consider the needs of cross-border workers and take action to improve road safety.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete Actions

To address the situation of pedestrian crossings in Como, it is necessary for local authorities to adopt a series of concrete measures. Firstly, it is essential to launch a regular maintenance program to repaint pedestrian crossings throughout the city. This program should be planned and implemented urgently to ensure the safety of pedestrians.

### Reporting Procedure

Citizens and cross-border workers can contribute to solving the problem by reporting damaged or missing pedestrian crossings to local authorities. Reports can be submitted through the website of the Municipality of Como or by directly contacting the competent office. Reports should include photos and precise indications of the location of the damaged pedestrian crossings.

### Community Involvement

Community involvement is fundamental to solving the problem of pedestrian crossings. Citizens and cross-border workers can organize local initiatives to raise public awareness and urge action from the authorities. Additionally, it is possible to participate in public meetings and meetings with local representatives to discuss the situation and propose solutions.

### Use of Site Tools

To delve deeper into the situation and find concrete solutions, you can use the tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. The [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help assess the economic impact of transportation and maintenance costs, while the [cost comparator](nav:cost-of-living) can provide useful information on the quality of life and road safety in different cities.

### Conclusion

The report on pedestrian crossings in Como highlights a critical road safety issue that requires urgent intervention. Local authorities must act promptly to repaint pedestrian crossings and ensure the safety of all citizens. Cross-border workers and citizens can contribute by reporting critical situations and actively participating in community life. By using the tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website, it is possible to find concrete solutions and improve the quality of life in the city of Como.

item.title: Varese among Europe's greenest cities
item.description: Varese is one of only two European cities where over 50% of the population lives by the 3-30-300 rule.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Varese is among the greenest cities in Europe
- Over 50% of the population respects the 3-30-300 rule
- Only 13.5% of European citizens live in similar conditions

## Key facts
- **What**: Study on 862 European cities
- When: Published in Nature Communications
- Where: Varese, Italy
- Who: Nature and JRC
- Amount: 50% of Varese's population respects the 3-30-300 rule

Varese has been recognized as one of the greenest cities in Europe according to a study published in Nature Communications. The research, which analyzed 862 cities on the continent, used the 3-30-300 rule developed by educator Cecil Konijnendijk. This rule states that every resident should have three trees visible from their window, live in a neighborhood with at least 30% tree cover, and be no more than 300 meters from an accessible park or green area.

Varese, along with Espoo in Finland, is one of only two European cities where more than 50% of the population lives in full compliance with these criteria. This achievement places the Lombard city at the top of the continent, ahead of capitals like Stockholm, Helsinki, and Berlin.

### Comparison with other Italian cities
In Italy, Varese tops the national ranking alongside Reggio Emilia, Pavia, and Perugia. In these cities, over 24% of residents live in neighborhoods that meet the 3-30-300 rule parameters. Conversely, in southern regions, most municipalities have less than 2% of residents with uniform access to green spaces.

The study highlights that overall compliance with the rule does not depend solely on the score achieved in individual criteria but also on the spatial distribution of green areas. Varese offers a rare insight: being cited in a continental-scale scientific analysis not as a problem to be solved, but as a model to be studied.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who commute to Ticino and reside in Lombardy, Varese represents an example of how sustainable urban planning can improve quality of life. The presence of accessible and well-distributed green areas can positively influence overall well-being and productivity.

### Event with architect Elena Granata
On May 18th at Materia, there will be an event with architect Elena Granata, who will present her book 'La città è di tutti'. The professor from the Politecnico di Milano will dedicate a large part of her work to environmental themes.

Varese, with its reputation built over time through the coexistence of city and nature, finally has a rigorous and comparable measure at the European scale. This recognition can encourage other cities to follow Varese's example and systematically and extensively integrate green spaces into urban planning. ## Practical Analysis
The 3-30-300 rule not only improves the quality of life but also has a significant impact on the environment and public health. Varese, with its high percentage of tree cover and access to parks and green areas, offers a model of urban sustainability that can be adopted in other cities as well.

### Benefits for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers living and working in urban areas, the presence of accessible green spaces can significantly improve the quality of life. The ability to easily access parks and green areas can reduce stress and improve mental health, as well as promote a more active and healthy lifestyle.

### Comparison with Other European Cities
Varese and Espoo are the only two cities in Europe where more than 50% of the population adheres to the 3-30-300 rule. This comparison highlights the importance of urban planning that takes into account the equitable distribution of public green spaces. Cities that fail to ensure equitable access to nature risk creating concrete inequalities, not only environmental but also social and economic.

### Future Challenges
The challenge for the coming decades is to integrate green spaces into urban planning in a systematic and widespread manner, reaching even the most marginalized neighborhoods. This requires concrete commitment from local authorities and close collaboration with urban planning and environmental experts.

### Events and Initiatives
Events such as the one with architect Elena Granata represent an opportunity to discuss and promote sustainable practices in urban contexts. The presentation of the book 'La città è di tutti' can offer interesting insights to improve the quality of life in cities, not only for residents but also for cross-border workers living and working in these areas.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions
For cross-border workers who want to live in a sustainable urban environment, Varese sets a positive example. Here are some concrete actions that can be taken to improve the quality of life and promote sustainability:

1. Participate in local events and initiatives: Events like the one with architect Elena Granata offer the opportunity to learn and discuss sustainable practices in urban contexts.
2. **Use local green spaces**: Take advantage of parks and green areas accessible for recreational and sports activities.
3. Promote sustainability: Support local initiatives that promote sustainability and equitable urban planning.
4. Collaborate with local authorities: Participate in projects and initiatives aimed at improving the distribution of public green spaces.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers who want to plan their move or improve their quality of life in the cities where they live, tools like the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can be used to compare the costs and benefits of living in sustainable cities.

### Conclusion
Varese represents a model of urban sustainability that can be adopted in other cities as well. For cross-border workers, living in a sustainable urban environment can significantly improve the quality of life and promote a healthier and more active lifestyle. Participating in local events and using accessible green spaces are concrete actions that can make a difference.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/varese-e-tra-le-citta-piu-verdi-deuropa/2561911/)*

item.title: Varese Province Budget: 1.5M for Investments
item.description: The 2025 budget for Varese Province closes with a surplus of 1.5 million, earmarked for investments in road infrastructure and cultural heritage.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 2025 budget approved with a surplus of 1.5 million
- Investments in road infrastructure and cultural heritage
- 500,000 euros for the former Politeama cinema

## Key facts
- **What**: Province of Varese 2025 Budget
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Province of Varese
- Who: President Magrini and Provincial Council
- Amount: 1,594,244.40 euros surplus allocated to investments

The 2025 budget of the Province of Varese closed with a positive result, respecting all major economic and financial balances. President Magrini expressed satisfaction with the solid accounts and the Entity's ability to transform resources into concrete interventions for the territory. The report showed a free administration surplus of over 17,009,730.80 euros and an investment surplus of 1,594,244.40 euros.

### Strategic investments

The Provincial Council approved an amendment to the 2026-2028 budget forecast for 2026, aimed at financing interventions in strategic sectors for the territory. This maneuver fits into a broader strategy of the Provincial Administration aimed at strengthening the safety of the road network, supporting the Municipalities and enhancing the heritage as a lever for territorial development.

Among the most significant investments, over 840,000 euros are allocated to the road connection in the Municipality of Travedona Monate, between the provincial road SP 32 and the state road 629. Furthermore, more than 175,000 euros have been allocated for the purchase of equipment for Civil Protection. Another important investment is represented by the 500,000 euros of free surplus destined for the enhancement of cultural assets, in particular for the redevelopment works of the former Politeama cinema in the Municipality of Varese.

### New funding

Among the most significant results, the obtaining of new funding, including a regional contribution of 950,000 euros that will allow the enhancement of Villa Campiotti, at the Abbey of San Gemolo in Ganna. Also important are the choices regarding the transport and road network plan: the amendment provides for safety interventions on bridges and roads for over 700,000 euros, financed through ministerial and regional funds.

President Magrini emphasized that, in a context of limited resources, the Province continues to play a fundamental role of coordination and support to the Municipalities, translating every available resource into concrete interventions. Safety in schools, on the roads and of our citizens is a priority issue for the Administration, for this reason investments will continue in this direction.

The amendment was declared immediately executable in order to allow the timely start of interventions on the territory. ## Implications for cross-border workers

The investments planned in the budget of the Province of Varese will have a significant impact on cross-border workers who live and work in the area. In particular, improvements to road infrastructure and the safety of bridges and roads will contribute to improving the quality of life and safety of daily commutes.

### Improvements in road infrastructure

The road connection in the municipality of Travedona Monate, funded with over 840,000 euros, represents an important step forward for local traffic. This intervention will improve connectivity between the provincial road SP 32 and the state road 629, facilitating the movements of cross-border workers who use these arteries to reach their workplaces or residences.

### Enhancement of cultural heritage

The enhancement of cultural assets, such as the former Politeama cinema in the municipality of Varese, will contribute to creating a more attractive and livable environment. These interventions can promote the development of cultural and recreational activities, offering new opportunities for leisure and socialization for cross-border workers and their families.

### Support for municipalities

Investments in Civil Protection and support for municipalities represent an additional element of security and stability for the territory. Cross-border workers will benefit from greater attention to safety and maintenance of infrastructure, improving overall quality of life.

### Comparisons with the previous situation

Compared to the previous situation, the 2025 budget of the Province of Varese shows a significant improvement in investments in infrastructure and cultural heritage. This represents an important step forward for the territory and its inhabitants, including cross-border workers who daily cross the border to work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino.

### Future scenarios

With the timely start of the planned interventions, a tangible improvement in the quality of life and safety in the territory is expected. Cross-border workers will benefit from more efficient traffic and a safer and more attractive environment, contributing to strengthening the bond between local and cross-border communities.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What cross-border workers can do

For cross-border workers who wish to take advantage of the new opportunities offered by the Province of Varese's investments, it is important to stay informed and actively participate in the life of the territory. Here are some concrete actions that can be taken:

### Participate in cultural initiatives

With the enhancement of assets such as the former Politeama cinema, cross-border workers can participate in cultural and recreational events that will enrich their experience of living in the territory. It is possible to consult the local event calendars and actively participate in the activities proposed.

### Utilize new road infrastructures

Interventions on road traffic, such as the road connection in the municipality of Travedona Monate, will improve the quality of daily commutes. Cross-border workers can plan their routes based on the new infrastructures, taking advantage of the improved connections between the different areas of the territory.

### Monitor the developments of Civil Protection

With the purchase of new equipment for Civil Protection, cross-border workers can feel safer and more protected. It is important to stay updated on the initiatives and training courses offered, to be ready to contribute to the community's safety.

### Consult official documents

To stay informed about the developments of the Province of Varese's investments and initiatives, cross-border workers can consult official documents and participate in public assemblies. This will allow them to always be up-to-date on the news and actively participate in the life of the territory.

### Use the salary calculator

To best plan their finances and take advantage of the new opportunities offered by the territory, cross-border workers can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) available on the site. This tool allows calculating taxes and pension contributions, facilitating the management of personal finances.

Conclusion

The investments planned in the budget of the Province of Varese represent an important opportunity for cross-border workers who work and live in the territory. By actively participating in the life of the territory and taking advantage of the new infrastructures and initiatives, cross-border workers can improve their quality of life and contribute to the well-being of the community.

item.title: Medical guard active for the 1st May bank holiday: what to know
item.description: Continuity of Care active until 8 AM on Monday 4th May. Saturday Paediatric Clinic active on 2nd May. Emergency number 116 117.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Guardian medical service active until 8 AM on Monday, May 4th
- Pediatric Clinic active on May 2nd
- Green number 116 117 for both services

## Key facts
- **What**: Continuity of Care and Pediatric Clinic
- When: From May 1st to May 4th
- Where: Como and province
- Who: ASST Lariana
- Cost: Free services

On the occasion of the May 1st bank holiday, the Continuity of Care service in Como will be active until 8 AM on Monday, May 4th. This free service can be accessed by calling the green number 116 117. Additionally, the Continuity of Care service is enhanced by the Saturday Pediatric Clinic, active on May 2nd. For this free service as well, it is necessary to contact the number 116 117.

### What is Continuity of Care

The Continuity of Care service is activated by calling the number 116 117 every day from 7 PM to 8 AM and 24/7 on holidays and eve of holidays. The doctors of the Continuity of Care provide a service for situations of illness that cannot be postponed to your own family doctor. They offer telephone advice or a teleconsultation if the problem can be resolved in this way, or they invite the patient to reach the nearest Continuity of Care location for an outpatient evaluation. If they deem it necessary, the doctors can carry out a home visit or activate the Emergency Service.

### What is the pediatric clinic

The pediatric clinic is a service active every Saturday (non-holiday) dedicated to minors residing/domiciled in the territory of competence of ASST Lariana. It is indicated for all those cases that require non-urgent clinical advice and evaluation. On Saturday, from 8 AM to 6 PM, call the number 116 117 and a first information sheet is drawn up regarding the problem. The family is then called back by the staff who, if necessary, schedule an appointment for the afternoon (between 2 PM and 7 PM) in the appropriate clinic. The child will then be visited by a pediatrician of free choice who will carry out the clinical evaluation of the case, providing any prescriptions/prescriptions. In the activity, the doctor is assisted by staff of ASST Lariana (nurse expert in the management of pediatric patients).

Each clinic has about 20 appointments available for visits that are scheduled for the afternoon. Once the agenda is full, calls are automatically redirected from the central office to the Continuity of Care location of territorial competence (i.e., the one closest to the residence/domicile of the caller). The active locations are in Cantù, via Domea, 4, in building P, the building that houses the Community House (entering it is the first one on the left) and in Fino Mornasco in via Trieste 5, in the Continuity of Care location. ### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Como, it's important to know that the Continuity of Care service is free and accessible via the toll-free number 116 117. This service is particularly useful for those who cannot reach their primary care physician during holidays or bank holidays. Additionally, the Saturday Pediatric Clinic offers a valuable service for parents who need non-urgent pediatric advice.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before this initiative, cross-border workers often faced difficulties in finding medical assistance during holidays. With the activation of Continuity of Care and the Saturday Pediatric Clinic, cross-border workers can now rely on a more accessible and free service. This represents a significant improvement in the quality of healthcare offered to residents and cross-border workers.

### Practical scenarios

Imagine a scenario where a cross-border worker residing in Ticino and working in Como needs medical assistance during the 1st May bank holiday. Thanks to Continuity of Care, they can call the toll-free number 116 117 and receive immediate assistance. If they have a child who needs non-urgent pediatric advice, they can use the Saturday Pediatric Clinic, calling the same toll-free number and following the described procedure.

### Concrete procedures

To access the Continuity of Care and Pediatric Clinic services, follow these steps:

1. Call the toll-free number 116 117.
2. Provide information about the health issue.
3. Follow the instructions given by the operator for ambulatory evaluation or home visit.
4. For the Pediatric Clinic, wait for the callback to schedule an appointment in the afternoon.

By following these steps, cross-border workers can ensure access to quality healthcare services for themselves and their children during holidays and bank holidays.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Como, it is essential to know about the Continuity of Care and Pediatric Clinic services available during the 1st May bank holiday. Here's what to do:

1. **Inform yourself**: Carefully read the information provided by ASST Lariana about the available services.
2. Prepare: Make sure you have the green number 116 117 at hand to contact the service if needed.
3. Plan ahead: If you anticipate needing medical assistance during the 1st May bank holiday, plan in advance and contact the green number to schedule an appointment.
4. Use the services: In case of need, do not hesitate to call the green number 116 117 to access the Continuity of Care and Pediatric Clinic services.

### Useful tools

For further information and to best plan access to healthcare services during the 1st May bank holiday, cross-border workers can use the [healthcare cost calculator](nav:health) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to estimate the costs associated with healthcare services and plan your expenses effectively.

### Final CTA

For more details and to best plan access to healthcare services during the 1st May bank holiday, visit the [healthcare cost calculator](nav:health) on Frontaliere Ticino. This tool will help you estimate costs and plan your expenses effectively.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/sanita/como-per-il-ponte-del-1-maggio-e-attiva-la-guardia-medica-le-cose-importanti-da-sapere/)*

item.title: Farewell to Salandin, historic Cgil leader
item.description: The Varese trade union world is in mourning for the passing of Giovanni Salandin, a historic Cgil leader who dedicated his life to defending workers' rights.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Giovanni Salandin, a historic Cgil leader, has passed away.
- Final farewell in Brissago Valtravaglia on April 30, 2026.
- He curated the 'Rido!' exhibition for the Cgil centennial.

## Key facts
- **What**: Passing of Giovanni Salandin, historic Cgil leader.
- When: April 30, 2026.
- Where: Brissago Valtravaglia, Ticino.
- Who: Cgil of Varese and cross-border workers.
- Amount: Not specified.

The Varese trade union world is in mourning for the passing of Giovanni Salandin, a historic leader of the Cgil who marked decades of struggles and cultural initiatives in the territory. A respected figure for his balance and dedication, Salandin managed to combine the defense of workers' rights with a deep passion for information and training, leaving an indelible mark on various categories, from graphic workers to cross-border commuters.

Giovanni Salandin's career began directly in the field, as a union delegate at the Plv cartotecnica in Daverio. In that productive environment, he became known and appreciated by colleagues and coworkers for his constant commitment, which led him, shortly after the mid-1980s, to become the provincial secretary of Filis, the federation that brought together workers in information and entertainment.

A crucial step in his career occurred in 1996 when he was elected general secretary of Slc, the new name born from the unification between Filis and the Postelegrafonici. It was a complex task that Salandin faced with great dedication, managing to bring together very different working cultures. At the same time, he never forgot the importance of transmitting values to new generations, regularly going to Enaip in Varese to teach students the basics of union rights.

### Protection of cross-border workers and the 'Rido!' exhibition
Between 2001 and 2007, Salandin joined the confederation apparatus specifically dealing with cross-border workers, a vital sector for the economy of the province of Varese. Beyond the contractual aspect, he knew how to give space to creativity: in 2006, on the occasion of the Cgil centennial, he curated, together with Franco Stasi and Tiziano Riverso, the cartoon exhibition-contest 'Rido!'. The initiative involved personalities such as Sergio Staino, Gad Lerner, and Roberto Vecchioni, later becoming a book that traveled through congresses all over Italy.

The news of his passing has caused deep emotion among the leaders and members of the union. 'The Secretariat of the Cgil of Varese - the organization's message of condolence - expresses its condolences to the family of Giovanni Salandin, remembering his great passion for union commitment and his ability to create many initiatives capable of enriching the life of our organization.' Giovanni Salandin's commitment to cross-border workers has been fundamental for the economy of the province of Varese. His dedication has improved contractual and working conditions for many cross-border workers, who represent a significant part of the workforce in the region.

The 'Rido!' exhibition was a major cultural initiative that involved important figures from the world of culture and information. This project not only celebrated the centenary of the CGIL but also contributed to raising public awareness about workers' rights and the importance of trade unions.

### Practical implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers who have benefited from Salandin's work can continue to refer to the contractual achievements obtained thanks to his commitment. It is important that workers are aware of their rights and the trade union protections available, especially in a complex cross-border context like that between Italy and Switzerland.

For young people who wish to pursue a trade union path, Salandin's example represents a model of dedication and passion. His experience in teaching young students at ENAIP Varese demonstrates the importance of transmitting trade union values to new generations, thus ensuring the continuity of the commitment to workers' rights.

### Comparisons and scenarios
Before Salandin's arrival, the situation of cross-border workers was more precarious, with fewer contractual protections and fewer opportunities for training. Thanks to his work, significant improvements have been achieved, making cross-border work safer and more dignified.

In a context where workers' rights are often at risk, Salandin's legacy represents an important point of reference. His ability to unite different working cultures within the SLC demonstrates how it is possible to find shared solutions to improve working conditions for everyone.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). For cross-border workers who wish to delve deeper into union protections and labor rights, it is possible to consult the guides and services offered by the Cgil. In particular, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can be useful for better understanding one's economic and contractual conditions.

### Procedure to request union assistance
1. **Contact the Cgil in Varese**: Cross-border workers can turn to the local Cgil office to receive assistance and information about their rights.
2. Consult online guides: The Cgil offers a series of guides and online resources that can help workers better understand union protections.
3. Attend training courses: As Salandin did, it is important to participate in training courses to acquire union skills and improve one's working conditions.

### Useful tools
- [Salary calculator](nav:calculator): To calculate your net salary and better understand contractual protections.
- [Union guides](nav:jobs): To delve into the protections and rights of workers.
- [Assistance services](nav:residency): To receive legal and contractual support.

The legacy of Giovanni Salandin represents an example of commitment and dedication that can inspire today's and tomorrow's workers. His passion for union rights and his ability to unite different working cultures remain a model for all those who wish to improve the working conditions and lives of cross-border workers.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/addio-a-giovanni-salandin-storico-leader-cgil-lultimo-saluto-a-brissago-valtravaglia/2562052/)*

item.title: ATS Insubria: 398 men followed in violence perpetrator centers
item.description: In the first year of activity, the Centers for Male Perpetrators of Violence (CUAV) followed 398 men, with an average age of 42 years.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 398 men followed in the CUAV in the first year
- 224 completed the assessment process
- Average age of 42 years

## Key facts
- **What**: Balance of the Centers for Men Authors of Violence (CUAV)
- When: First year of activity
- Where: Territories of ATS Insubria (Varese and Como)
- Who: ATS Insubria, CIPM, Dorian Gray Onlus, Associazione InfraMente, Fondazione Somaschi
- Amount: 398 men intercepted, 224 completed the process

In the first year of activity, the Centers for Men Authors or potential authors of Violence (CUAV) active in the territories of ATS Insubria outline a first balance: 398 men intercepted between requests for information and initial interviews, of which 224 completed the assessment process. The four active sites between Varese and Como mainly welcome men with an average age of 42, often already involved in judicial proceedings related to episodes of domestic violence against partners, ex-partners and, in some cases, children.

### Paths of accountability
The data from this first year demonstrate how fundamental it is to work on the protection of victims and also on the change of men who are authors of violence. Intervening on both fronts means preventing new risky situations and contributing to the creation of more aware and safer communities. Centers offer individual and group paths to help recognize the violence acted, take responsibility for it and develop alternative relational modalities.

### Prevention and territorial work
Alongside the treatment paths, ample space is dedicated to prevention: meetings in schools on the themes of consent and gender stereotypes, activities with people with addictions and public events. The work continues in the prisons of Varese and Como, with groups addressed to the detainees. The CUAV are managed on the territory by CIPM, Dorian Gray Onlus, Associazione InfraMente and Fondazione Somaschi.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino, it is important to know that these centers offer support not only to the authors of violence, but also to the victims. The geographical proximity and the collaboration between Italian and Swiss institutions facilitate access to services, making the prevention and treatment of domestic violence more effective. ## Analysis of Practical Implications
The Centers for Men Authors of Violence (CUAV) represent a significant step in the fight against domestic violence, offering concrete support to both perpetrators and victims. For cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino, the geographical proximity and collaboration between Italian and Swiss institutions facilitate access to services, making the prevention and treatment of domestic violence more effective.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before the establishment of the CUAV, the management of domestic violence was often fragmented and less coordinated. The creation of these centers has allowed resources to be centralized and more structured and targeted treatment paths to be offered. This integrated approach not only helps perpetrators recognize and manage their violence but also contributes to protecting victims and preventing new risky situations.

### Concrete Scenarios
Imagine a cross-border worker who works in Varese and resides in Mendrisio. If this individual is involved in a domestic violence incident, they can turn to the CUAV to receive support and follow a path of empowerment. Similarly, victims can access protection and assistance services, ensuring a safer and more aware environment for everyone.

### Collaboration Between Institutions
The collaboration between ATS Insubria, CIPM, Dorian Gray Onlus, Associazione InfraMente, and Fondazione Somaschi is fundamental to ensuring an integrated and coordinated approach. This synergy allows for more comprehensive services to be offered and a greater number of people in need of support to be reached.

### Prevention and Awareness
In addition to treatment paths, the CUAV dedicates ample space to prevention through meetings in schools, activities with people with addictions, and public events. These initiatives aim to raise awareness in the community about the issues of consent and gender stereotypes, contributing to creating a more aware and safe society.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions and useful tools
For cross-border workers who want to delve deeper or access the services offered by the Centres for Men Perpetrators of Violence (CUAV), it is important to know the procedures and available resources. Here is a practical guide to navigating this context.

### Procedure to access services
1. **Contact the CUAV**: The centers are active in the locations of Varese and Como. It is possible to request information or schedule a first interview by phone or through the website.
2. Initial assessment: During the first interview, the specific situation will be evaluated and a personalized path will be defined.
3. Participation in paths: The men involved can participate in individual or group paths, aimed at recognizing and managing violence.
4. Continuous support: The centers offer continuous support to ensure change and prevent relapses.

### Useful tools
- Salary calculator: For cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino, it is important to correctly calculate their salary and taxes. Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an accurate estimate.
- Tax guide: Compare tax rates between Italy and Switzerland to optimize your financial situation. Consult the [tax guide](nav:tax-return) for more details.
- Legal assistance: In case of need, you can turn to legal assistance services to protect your rights and those of the victims.

### Deadlines and required documents
There are no specific deadlines to access the services of the CUAV, but it is important to act promptly in case of need. Useful documents include: identity, residence documents and any judicial documents.

### Conclusion
The Centres for Men Perpetrators of Violence (CUAV) represent an important resource for the prevention and treatment of domestic violence. For cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino, access to these services is facilitated by geographical proximity and collaboration between institutions. Use the available tools to calculate your salary and optimize your financial situation, and do not hesitate to contact the CUAV in case of need.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/ats-insubria-fa-il-primo-bilancio-dei-centri-per-autori-di-violenza-contro-le-donne-398-uomini-presi-in-carico/2562649/)*

item.title: May Day 2026: history, accidents and work transformations
item.description: Stefania Filetti, General Secretary of the CGIL of Varese, retraces the history of May Day and analyzes the data on workplace accidents in the province.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The first of May celebrates workers, not work.
- In 1886, in Chicago, six workers died during a strike for an eight-hour workday.
- In Italy, the first of May was celebrated clandestinely during fascism.

## Key facts
- **What**: International Workers' Day
- When: 1889 (Second International Socialist), 1886 (Chicago strike)
- Where: Varese, Italy
- Who: Stefania Filetti, CGIL Varese
- Amount: 416,900 work-related injury reports nationwide in 2025

On the eve of May Day 2026, Stefania Filetti, general secretary of CGIL Varese, retraced the historical origins of International Workers' Day. The holiday, born in 1889 during the Second International Socialist in Paris, has deep roots in the decades of the industrial revolution. Working conditions at the time were harsh: fourteen, sixteen hours a day, one day of weekly rest, child labor, unsanitary environments, and insufficient wages. These factors led to the awareness of the importance of work and fair compensation.
